ordinalsimr 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    Coding Simulations
  • Changelog

Run Simulations

Source: R/run_simulations.R
run_simulations.Rd

Run Simulations

Usage

run_simulations(sample_size, sample_prob, prob0, prob1, niter)

Arguments

sample_size

Total number of trial participants

sample_prob

a vector of probability weights for obtaining the elements of the vector being sampled.

prob0

Vector of probabilities for control group

prob1

Vector of probabilities for intervention group

niter

Number of simulation iterations to complete#'

Value

a list of lists; sub-list elements include `p_values` which is a matrix of p values for tests at each iteration, and `initial_groups` which is the group assignment information for each iteration

Examples

run_simulations(sample_size = c(40,50,60),
sample_prob = c(0.5,0.5),
prob0 = c(0.1,0.2,0.3,0.4),
prob1 = c(0.6,0.2,0.1,0.1),
niter = 100)
#> Warning: cannot compute exact p-value with ties
#> Warning: Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect
#> Warning: Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect
#> Warning: cannot compute exact p-value with ties
#> Warning: Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect
#> Warning: Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect

#> $sample_size_40
#> # A tibble: 100 × 14
#>        wilcox    fisher chi_sq_false chi_sq_true     lrm kruskal coinasymp   run
#>         <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> <int>
#>  1 0.000695     3.07e-4    0.00134     0.00134   3.10e-4 6.60e-4   6.60e-4     1

#>  7 0.000486     2.05e-3    0.00540     0.00540   2.29e-4 4.60e-4   4.60e-4     7
#>  8 0.00724      3.11e-2    0.0315      0.0315    5.07e-3 6.94e-3   6.94e-3     8

#> 10 0.00000169   7.49e-7    0.0000144   0.0000144 7.43e-8 1.57e-6   1.57e-6    10
#> # ℹ 90 more rows

#> #   sample_size <dbl>, K <int>
#>
